assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a 'Version Impact Table' (lines 40-48) listing all 7 supported versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4). Every row shows Affected? as '**NO**' with serde_json versions ranging from 1.0.137 to 1.0.139, all above the fix threshold of 1.0.135. The summary states: 'No supported versions are affected.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 7 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md header reads 'Decision: Close as Not a Bug (Not Affected)' and states 'This is Case C per the triage-security skill: no supported versions are affected.' Proposed Jira action #4 (line 99) is: 'jira.transition_issue("TC-8002", resolution="Not a Bug")'. Section 'No Remediation Tasks Required' (line 118) explicitly states: 'Since no supported versions are affected, no remediation tasks are created. This is Case C of the triage-security skill's remediation decision tree.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md proposed action #2 'Add triage comment' (lines 58-82) includes a full version impact table in the comment body listing all 7 versions with their exact serde_json versions: 2.1.0=1.0.137, 2.1.1=1.0.137, 2.2.0=1.0.138, 2.2.1=1.0.138, 2.2.2=1.0.138, 2.2.3=1.0.139, 2.2.4=1.0.139, all marked NO. The comment also explains: 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.137 which is outside the affected range (versions before 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 7 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md proposed action #3 'Set VEX Justification' (lines 86-95) proposes: jira.edit_issue("TC-8002", fields={"customfield_12345": "Component not Present"}). The VEX field (customfield_12345) is identified as configured in data-extraction.md (line 7). The rationale explains: 'The vulnerable component (serde_json < 1.0.135) is not present in any shipped product version.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 7 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md explicitly states in the 'No Remediation Tasks Required' section (lines 118-120): 'Since no supported versions are affected, no remediation tasks are created. This is Case C of the triage-security skill's remediation decision tree.' The 'Cross-Stream Impact' section (lines 122-124) confirms: 'Neither stream is affected. No cross-stream remediation or notifications are required.' The proposed Jira actions list only: remove Affects Version, add comment, set VEX, transition to Closed, assign, add label — no task creation actions.
